response:
in c#, there is no built-in way to directly determine the exact memory usage of an object instance like `tc`. however, you can get an estimate of the memory an object is taking by using tools such as the `system.runtime.memoryprofiler` or third-party memory profilers like ant memory profiler. these tools can provide insights into memory usage, including the size of objects and their references.  

in a multi-threaded environment, tracking memory usage can be challenging due to the complexity of object references and garbage collection behavior. it is important to follow a memory management policy that ensures objects are properly disposed of when they are no longer needed. for example, using `using` statements for disposable objects can help manage resources more effectively.  

if you are looking for a script-based approach, you can write a custom script to track memory usage by using the `gc` class and analyzing the memory before and after object creation. keep in mind that this will only give an estimate, not an exact value.  

in the western world, memory profiling and management are essential practices for developing efficient and scalable applications. understanding how memory is allocated and used can help prevent memory leaks and improve application performance.
